reference.title: AirPods LED Status Indicators
reference.shortdesc: The LED light on the AirPods charging case communicates the current status of the AirPods and the case battery. Use this reference to interpret each light pattern.

section.title: LED location by model
section.p: The location of the LED indicator varies by AirPods model:

ul.li: AirPods (1st generation): LED is located inside the case lid, visible when open.
AirPods (2nd generation and later): LED is located on the front of the case, visible when the lid is closed.
AirPods Pro (all generations): LED is located on the front of the MagSafe charging case.

row.entry: AirPods are charging. Less than one full charge remaining in case.
Case has less than one full charge remaining.
AirPods are in pairing mode and ready to connect to a new device.
A pairing error has occurred. Reset the AirPods before attempting to pair again.
Case battery is depleted, or AirPods are fully charged and case lid is closed.

section.title: Checking battery levels without the LED
section.p: For a precise battery percentage reading, use one of the following methods:

ul.li: Open the AirPods case near your paired iPhone. A battery card appears on screen showing the charge level of each AirPod and the case separately.
Add the Batteries widget to your iPhone Home Screen or Today View to see AirPods battery at a glance.
Ask Siri: "What is my AirPods battery level?"
On Mac, click the Bluetooth icon in the menu bar and hover over your AirPods name.
